channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 How Ukraine Became a Drone Superpower | Just Security
item.description: Ukraine is rewriting the rules of air power, replacing stockpiles of weapons as key factors in warfare with quantity, speed, and the ability to learn faster than the enemy. The post How Ukraine Became a Drone Superpower appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ Army vet supports her Navy sister in tough hospital journey | Defense News
item.description: When Navy fire controlman aegis Kayla Saska needed support during a hospital stay, her Army veteran sister Patricia Rowledge rushed to Bethesda, Maryland.

item.title: 📍 US-Caribbean maritime cooperation: Why stronger ports, supply chains, and security matter now | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Caribbean is not a peripheral region but a core component of the United States' economic and security environment. Its ports support US trade, its routes shape regional stability, and its infrastructure is now central to geopolitical competition. The post US-Caribbean…

item.title: 📍 The small business making big investments to deliver advanced defense technologies | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Employee-owned Physical Sciences Inc. has been scaling small business government R&D awards to solve some of the thorniest national security challenges.

item.title: 🔐 “Hunting” the October 7 Attackers: What the Law of Armed Conflict Permits – and What It Doesn’t | Just Security
item.description: Legal analysis of Israel's operation to kill or capture every individual involved in the October 7th massacre. The post “Hunting” the October 7 Attackers: What the Law of Armed Conflict Permits – and What It Doesn’t appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Just Security’s Israel-Hamas War Archive | Just Security
item.description: Just Security's collection of more than 110 articles covering the Israel-Hamas War and its diplomatic, legal, and humanitarian consequences. The post Just Security’s Israel-Hamas War Archive appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 1, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated guide to major news and developments over the weekend. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE President Trump on Friday requested several changes to a proposed deal to extend the existing ceasefire with Iran…

item.title: 🏴 Poland spends $16.5 billion in EU-backed loans on heavy army weapons | Defense News
item.description: Amid political infighting over the funding mechanism, the government has tapped local companies to make fighting vehicles, howitzers and ammunition.

item.title: ⚛️ Norway becomes ninth country to sign up for French nuclear deterrence as trust in US falters | Defense News
item.description: Meanwhile, Germany will participate in French nuclear exercises as soon as September, joining them in an observer role.

item.title: 🌍 The Energy Crisis Will Long Outlast the Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: The baked-in damage to oil and gas production will take months to undo.

item.title: 📍 Germany Is Scrambling to Speed Up Its Rearmament | Foreign Policy
item.description: Berlin has already made a huge commitment to its military—but is facing new obstacles at home and abroad.

item.title: 🏴 Fences Not F-35s: Drone Attacks and the Illogic of Gulf Procurement | War on the Rocks
item.description: One of the most effective counter-drone systems in the largest drone war in history between Ukraine and Russia is a German anti-aircraft gun designed during the Cold War. The Gepard — a self-propelled 35 mm cannon that first entered service in 1976 — has earned recognition from…

item.title: 🏴 Deterring Russia Beneath the Waves: Securing NATO’s Critical Undersea Infrastructure | War on the Rocks
item.description: What would happen if you woke up one morning and the internet was gone — not from a software glitch, but because someone had simply cut the wire?Threats to critical undersea infrastructure are rapidly escalating. In 2023, the Balticonnector pipeline and several Baltic data…

item.title: 🌏 Philippines, Vietnam Upgrade Diplomatic Partnership Amid Maritime Tensions With China | The Diplomat
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. stated that maintaining peace, stability, and the freedom of navigation in the South China Sea "remains non-negotiable" for both nations.

item.title: 📍 Clinton’s Lincoln Bedroom Scandal Looks Quaint Now | Foreign Policy
item.description: What a very 1990s scandal says about corruption during the second Trump administration.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine Turns the Tide | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why a cease-fire is now a real possibility.

item.title: 📍 The End of Foreign Aid Is Not the End of Development | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How the world can do more with less.

item.title: 📍 How Migration Helps Authoritarians | Foreign Affairs
item.description: The costs of democratic drain.

item.title: 📍 What to Expect from the Impeachment Trial of the Philippine Vice President Duterte | The Diplomat
item.description: Various contending political blocs are looking to capitalize politically on Sara Duterte's trial as they look ahead to the presidential election of 2028.

item.title: 🌏 India Has Signed BrahMos Missile Deal With Vietnam, Indian Minister Says | The Diplomat
item.description: The country is set to become the third in Southeast Asia to purchase the powerful Indo-Russian missile system, after the Philippines and Indonesia.

item.title: 🌏 China’s expanding frontiers: views in The Strategist | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China appears to be playing the long game in pursuit of its interests in the Indo-Pacific. In this series of Strategist articles, Joe Keary, Raji Rajagopalan and Linus Cohen discuss how China is pursuing its regional ...

item.title: 🌏 Japan’s defense minister rebuffs ‘militarism’ allegation, defends defense policies | Breaking Defense
item.description: Shinjiro Koizumi says arms sales, more visible regional military presence aims to help partners defend themselves

item.title: 🌍 Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: What Do the Gulf States Really Want? | Atlantic Council
item.description: The post Charai for The Jerusalem Strategic Tribune: What Do the Gulf States Really Want? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ AUKUS partners sign agreement on underwater drones, speed up sub plan | Breaking Defense
item.description: Australia will now forgo the purchase of a new build Virginia-class submarine and acquire another ex-US Navy boat instead.

item.title: 🕵️ US general holds rare meeting with Cuban military officials near Guantanamo Bay | Defense News
item.description: The meeting is the first in recent memory by a head of U.S. Southern Command and comes amid growing concerns in Cuba of a possible U.S. military attack.

item.title: 📍 Timeline of Boat Strikes and Related Actions | Just Security
item.description: A timeline that chronicles major events in the Trump administration’s campaign of lethal strikes against suspected drug traffickers in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific. The post Timeline of Boat Strikes and Related Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🕵️ NDS 2026 – The right strategy for the wrong era | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Australian official thought regarding the defence of Australia has been more independently minded than is portrayed by certain academics and commentators who, often not possessing a deep understanding of the history of Australian strategy, have ...

item.title: 🌏 Hegseth praises Indo-Pacific nations for improving defense capabilities, pragmatism | Breaking Defense
item.description: Indo-Pacific countries investments in their defense an example of how burden sharing works, says Hegseth

item.title: 📍 Q&A with SOUTHCOM commander Gen. Frank Donovan | Defense One
item.description: "I don't really care about platforms. I care about autonomous warfare," says the former leader of the Defense Autonomous Warfare Group.

item.title: 🏴 How Russia is turning Ukraine’s drones against NATO | Defense News
item.description: A Russian drone wounded two civilians in Romania on Friday, days after Lithuania detailed how Moscow is also steering Ukraine's drones onto allied soil.
